rulings.Case Title: WINSTON S NICHOLS ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment By Defendant Fmc Corporation On Behalf Of Its Former Peerless Pump Business
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 24, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant FMC Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826 by demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence that Mr. Nichols was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. While plaintiffs' evidence is sufficient to create a triable issue whether Mr. Nichols was exposed to gaskets for which defendant (Peerless) is responsible, plaintiffs failed to submit admissible evidence sufficient to create a triable issue whether those gaskets contained asbestos. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Donald Keef are overruled and its objections to the declaration of Philip John Templin are sustained. Since defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong, the Court did not need to consider the declaration of Ret. Admiral Roger Horne, Jr. and therefore will not rule on plaintiffs' objections thereto. Plaintiffs' alternate request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. § 437c(h) is denied. (Opposition, p. 1, fn. 1.) Plaintiffs did not demonstrate, via affidavit or otherwise, that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot, for reasons stated, be presented. (C.C.P. § 437c(h).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/LOT)

Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar. Plaintiffs dismissed their claim for punitive damages against defendant. = (503/LOT)
